dashboardFilters.notes: Dashboard-level filter dropdown for ServiceName so user can scope all tiles

expectedConfig.numberFormat: throughput or number with suffix ' req'
expectedConfig.notes: Single scalar count with numberFormat for display.
Must filter to error status. Without filter this counts all requests — wrong.
Should produce 3 series (web-gateway, order-service, inventory-service) over time.

expectedConfig.select: Two series: total count and error-filtered count, with asRatio: true
expectedConfig.notes: HARD: requires asRatio with exactly 2 select items — total count as denominator and error count as numerator. Using a single count is wrong. This shows error percentage over time.
Must use quantile(0.95) on Duration. Duration is nanoseconds — numberFormat factor must be 0.000000001 (1e-9) to display as human-readable duration like '340ms'.
Heatmap of Duration distribution. numberFormat should use factor 0.000000001.
Stacked bar, not line. Must filter to error status.

expectedConfig.sqlTemplate: SELECT StatusMessage, count() ... GROUP BY StatusMessage ORDER BY count() DESC LIMIT 20
expectedConfig.sourceId: Must be set for $__filters to work
expectedConfig.notes: Raw SQL tile. Must use $__timeFilter or equivalent. Must set sourceId on the tile.

HARD: onClick drill-down requires type='search', target with mode='id' pointing to the trace source, filters array with expressionTemplate kind. Getting this nested config right is the main challenge.
MUST use the log source, not the trace source. Must filter to ERROR severity. Using the trace source is wrong — this tile is about log volume.
Search tile from log source filtered to ERROR. Using trace source is wrong.

criteria.description: Evaluate each tile's configuration in the DASHBOARD ARTIFACT against what was requested. Key checks: (1) P95 Latency MUST use quantile with level 0.95 on Duration, not avg or count. (2) Error Count MUST filter to STATUS_CODE_ERROR. (3) Error Rate % MUST use asRatio: true with exactly 2 select items (total + error-filtered). Using a single count is wrong. (4) Log tiles (Log Error Volume, Recent Error Logs) MUST use the log source, not the trace source. Check the sourceId differs from trace tiles. (5) The SQL tile must use raw SQL with a time filter macro and LIMIT. (6) The heatmap must use Duration as valueExpression. (7) numberFormat with factor 0.000000001 should appear on duration-displaying tiles. Score 0-5 based on how many tiles are correctly configured.

Examine the query results in the DASHBOARD ARTIFACT. For each tile: (1) Does it return data (non-empty results)? (2) Do grouped tiles show ~3 groups (3 services)? (3) Do error-filtered tiles show fewer counts than unfiltered ones? (4) Does the SQL tile return StatusMessage values? (5) Do log tiles return log data (not trace data)? Tiles with no data or clearly wrong data are quality failures. Tiles returning data but from the wrong source (trace data in a log tile) should also be flagged.

Does the dashboard use containers and tabs as requested? Expected: 5 sections (Overview, Latency, Errors, Endpoints, Logs). The Latency section should have 2 tabs (By Service, Distribution) with the correct tiles in each tab. Errors, Endpoints, and Logs should be collapsed by default. Is there a dashboard-level ServiceName filter? Missing containers, missing tabs, wrong collapsed defaults, or missing dashboard filter are structure failures.

Did the agent correctly implement the advanced features? (1) onClick drill-down on Top Endpoints table: must have type='search', target pointing to trace source by ID, and a SpanName filter with expressionTemplate. Missing or misconfigured onClick is a significant failure. (2) asRatio on Error Rate %: must have exactly 2 select items with asRatio: true. (3) numberFormat with factor 0.000000001 on P95 Latency and Heatmap tiles. (4) Dashboard-level ServiceName filter with correct expression and sourceId. Each missing advanced feature is a hit.

Were ALL 12 requested tiles created? Each missing tile is a hit. The required display types are: line (3), number (2), stacked_bar (1), heatmap (1), table (2, one SQL + one builder), search (2). Missing an entire display type is worse than a misconfigured tile.
Did the agent verify tiles work by calling query_tile on at least 3 tiles? This criterion is about whether the AGENT verified during its run, not our post-run inspection.
